childhood.familyBackground: Suburban Los Angeles upbringing; father was also an entrepreneur, providing business exposure from early age
childhood.familyBackgroundKo: 로스앤젤레스 교외 지역에서 성장했으며, 아버지도 기업가여서 어린 시절부터 사업에 노출될 수 있는 기회를 얻음
childhood.education: Studied computer engineering at UCLA (University of California, Los Angeles) but dropped out in 1998 to co-found Scour, Inc. with UCLA classmates
childhood.educationKo: UCLA(캘리포니아 대학교 로스앤젤레스)에서 컴퓨터공학을 전공했으나 1998년 UCLA 동기들과 함께 Scour, Inc.를 공동 창립하기 위해 중퇴
childhood.earlyLife: Interested in computers from young age; learned to write computer code by middle school. At 18, started test preparation company called 'New Way Academy'. Also worked selling knives door-to-door for Cutco company.
childhood.earlyLifeKo: 어린 나이부터 컴퓨터에 관심이 많았으며, 중학교 때 컴퓨터 코드를 작성하는 법을 배움. 18세에 '뉴웨이 아카데미(New Way Academy)'라는 입시 준비 회사 설립. 또한 Cutco 회사에서 칼을 직접 판매하는 일도 함.

careerTimeline.event: Founded 'New Way Academy,' a test preparation company, at age 18
careerTimeline.eventKo: 18세의 나이에 입시 준비 회사인 '뉴웨이 아카데미(New Way Academy)' 설립

careerTimeline.event: Co-founded Scour, Inc. with UCLA classmates; online file-exchange service; dropped out of UCLA to pursue venture
careerTimeline.eventKo: UCLA 동기들과 함께 온라인 파일 교환 서비스인 Scour, Inc. 공동 창립; UCLA에서 중퇴하고 사업 추진

careerTimeline.event: Scour sued for $250 billion by MPAA, RIAA, and NMPA for copyright infringement; filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in September 2000
careerTimeline.eventKo: Scour가 저작권 침해로 MPAA, RIAA, NMPA로부터 2,500억 달러의 소송 제기; 2000년 9월 11장 파산 신청

careerTimeline.event: Founded Red Swoosh with Michael Todd; peer-to-peer file-sharing company; called it his 'revenge business' against MPAA/RIAA
careerTimeline.eventKo: Michael Todd와 함께 P2P 파일 공유 회사인 Red Swoosh 설립; MPAA/RIAA에 대한 '복수 사업'이라고 표현

careerTimeline.event: Red Swoosh acquired by Akamai Technologies for approximately $19 million; Kalanick netted $2 million after taxes
careerTimeline.eventKo: Red Swoosh가 Akamai Technologies에 약 1,900만 달러에 인수됨; Kalanick은 세금 공제 후 200만 달러 수익

careerTimeline.event: Inspired by difficulty hailing taxi in Paris during tech conference; met Garrett Camp at LeWeb conference
careerTimeline.eventKo: 기술 컨퍼런스 중 파리에서 택시를 잡는 어려움에서 영감을 받음; LeWeb 컨퍼런스에서 Garrett Camp를 만남

careerTimeline.event: Co-founded Uber (originally UberCab) with Garrett Camp in March 2009; initially acted as 'mega advisor'; Ryan Graves hired as first CEO
careerTimeline.eventKo: 2009년 3월 Garrett Camp와 함께 Uber(원래는 UberCab)를 공동 창립; 초기에는 '메가 어드바이저'로 활동; Ryan Graves가 첫 CEO로 고용됨

careerTimeline.event: Became CEO of Uber after Graves removed; Camp and Graves signed over large portions of shares, giving Kalanick significant control
careerTimeline.eventKo: Graves 제거 후 Uber의 CEO가 됨; Camp와 Graves가 주식 대부분을 양도하여 Kalanick에게 상당한 지배권을 부여

careerTimeline.event: Uber valued at $18.2 billion; Kalanick officially became billionaire for first time; expanded to 66 countries and 360+ cities by 2015
careerTimeline.eventKo: Uber의 가치평가가 182억 달러에 달함; Kalanick이 공식적으로 억만장자가 됨; 2015년까지 66개 국가, 360개 이상의 도시로 확장

careerTimeline.event: Resigned as CEO in June 2017 under pressure from investors following sexual harassment allegations and workplace culture scandals
careerTimeline.eventKo: 2017년 6월 성희롱 혐의와 직장 문화 스캔들로 인한 투자자들의 압력으로 CEO 직에서 사임

careerTimeline.event: Left Uber's board; sold all Uber stock for more than $2.5 billion (pretax)
careerTimeline.eventKo: Uber 이사회에서 퇴출; 모든 Uber 주식을 25억 달러 이상에 판매(세전)

careerTimeline.event: Became CEO of CloudKitchens (ghost kitchen operator); personally invested $300 million; Saudi sovereign wealth fund invested $400 million
careerTimeline.eventKo: 클라우드 키친(CloudKitchens, 유령 주방 운영사)의 CEO가 됨; 개인적으로 3억 달러 투자; 사우디 국부펀드가 4억 달러 투자

failures.description: Scour, Inc. faced $250 billion lawsuit from MPAA, RIAA, and NMPA for copyright infringement. Company filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in September 2000. Michael Ovitz, an early investor and Hollywood mogul, withdrew support and actually joined the lawsuit against Scour.
failures.descriptionKo: Scour, Inc.가 저작권 침해로 MPAA, RIAA, NMPA로부터 2,500억 달러 소송에 직면. 2000년 9월 11장 파산 신청. 초기 투자자이자 할리우드 거물인 Michael Ovitz가 지원을 철회하고 Scour를 상대로 한 소송에 참여.
failures.lesson: Learned difficult lesson about intellectual property litigation and regulatory risk in peer-to-peer technologies. Realized importance of securing licensing agreements upfront rather than facing legal challenges.
failures.lessonKo: P2P 기술에서의 지적재산권 소송과 규제 위험에 대한 어려운 교훈을 배움. 법적 분쟁에 직면하기보다는 사전에 라이선스 계약을 확보하는 것의 중요성 깨달음.

failures.description: Red Swoosh faced difficulty securing funding as it launched right after the dot-com bubble burst. Struggled financially for years, surviving through series of last-minute investor deals and cash infusions.
failures.descriptionKo: Red Swoosh는 닷컴 버블 붕괴 직후 출범하여 자금 조달의 어려움을 겪음. 수년간 재정적으로 투쟁하며 최후의 순간 투자자 거래와 현금 주입을 통해 생존.
failures.lesson: Developed resilience through periods of near-bankruptcy and uncertainty. Learned to navigate investor relationships and funding cycles. Built determination to persevere despite setbacks.
failures.lessonKo: 파산 위기와 불확실성을 통해 회복력을 발전시킴. 투자자 관계와 자금 조달 사이클을 헤쳐나가는 법을 배움. 좌절에도 불구하고 계속 나아가려는 결단력을 키움.

failures.description: Forced to resign as Uber CEO due to sexual harassment allegations, workplace discrimination, and toxic company culture exposed by former engineer Susan Fowler. Perkins Coie investigation found 215 complaints (54 discrimination, 47 sexual harassment, 45 unprofessional behavior, 33 bullying). Failed to address or prevent systemic cultural issues.
failures.descriptionKo: 전직 엔지니어 Susan Fowler이 폭로한 성희롱 혐의, 직장 차별, 독성 회사 문화로 인해 Uber CEO 직에서 사임을 강요받음. Perkins Coie 조사에서 215건의 민원(차별 54건, 성희롱 47건, 부적절한 행동 45건, 괴롭힘 33건) 발견. 체계적 문화 문제를 해결하거나 방지하지 못함.
failures.lesson: Recognized need for personal transformation and leadership development. Later stated 'I must fundamentally change and grow up' and acknowledged he is 'a terrible person.' Took responsibility for allowing toxic culture to develop under his leadership despite denying knowledge of harassment reports.
failures.lessonKo: 개인적 변화와 리더십 개발의 필요성을 깨달음. 나중에 '나는 근본적으로 변해야 하고 성장해야 한다'고 밝히고 자신이 '끔찍한 사람'이라고 인정. 성희롱 보고를 몰랐다고 부인했음에도 불구하고 자신의 리더십 하에서 독성 문화가 발전하도록 허용한 책임을 짐.
failures.source: Business & Human Rights Centre, Washington Post, Fortune

quotes.text: When asked about a wife and a next wife: 'You're asking somebody who has a wife and is really happily married, "So, what's your next wife going to be like?"'
quotes.textKo: 아내와 다음 아내에 대해 질문을 받았을 때: '아내가 있고 정말 행복하게 결혼한 사람에게 "그럼 당신의 다음 아내는 어떨 거 같아요?"라고 물으신다고요?'
quotes.context: Controversial sexist analogy used to describe his commitment to Uber; later criticized as problematic
quotes.contextKo: Uber에 대한 자신의 헌신을 설명하기 위해 사용한 논쟁이 많은 성차별적 비유; 나중에 문제로 비판됨

quotes.text: It was a lifestyle thing. Me, my cofounder, and our hundred friends could roll around San Francisco like ballers.
quotes.textKo: 그것은 라이프스타일 문제였어요. 나, 내 공동 창립자, 그리고 우리의 백 명의 친구들이 샌프란시스코를 멋있게 돌아다닐 수 있었어요.
quotes.context: On how he conceptualized Uber's initial appeal and luxury positioning
quotes.contextKo: Uber의 초기 매력과 럭셔리 포지셔닝을 어떻게 개념화했는지에 대해

quotes.context: Self-assessment when confronted with controversies in 2017; showed moment of accountability
quotes.contextKo: 2017년 논란에 직면했을 때의 자기 평가; 책임감의 순간을 보여줌

quotes.text: The criticism we've received is a stark reminder that I must fundamentally change as a leader and grow up.
quotes.textKo: 우리가 받은 비판은 내가 리더로서 근본적으로 변해야 하고 성장해야 한다는 것을 명확하게 상기시켜 줍니다.
quotes.context: Internal memo to Uber employees apologizing for treating driver disrespectfully; public acknowledgment of need for personal transformation
quotes.contextKo: 운전자를 무례하게 대한 것에 대해 Uber 직원들에게 사과한 내부 메모; 개인적 변화 필요성에 대한 공개적 인정

quotes.text: I will see you in the trenches.
quotes.context: Told to a competitor CEO; indicated he viewed business as life-or-death competitive warfare similar to military conflict
quotes.contextKo: 경쟁 회사의 CEO에게 말한 말; 사업을 군사 분쟁과 유사한 생사가 걸린 경쟁 전쟁으로 봤음을 나타냄

authored.title: No published memoirs or business books; primarily known for Uber leadership rather than authorship
authored.note: Subject of multiple biographies including 'Super Pumped: The Battle for Uber' but has not authored his own business book
authored.noteKo: 'Super Pumped: The Battle for Uber'를 포함한 여러 전기의 주제이지만, 자신의 사업 서적을 저술하지 않음

recommended.title: The Fountainhead
recommended.author: Ayn Rand
recommended.why: Kalanick stated 'It's one of my favorite books'; reflects his interest in individualism and architecture; influenced his independent thinking
recommended.whyKo: Kalanick이 '내 가장 좋아하는 책 중 하나'라고 밝힘; 개인주의와 건축에 대한 관심을 반영; 독립적 사고에 영향을 미침

recommended.title: Atlas Shrugged
recommended.author: Ayn Rand
recommended.why: Mentioned in Washington Post interview; further indication of Rand's influence on Kalanick's philosophy of individual achievement and capitalism
recommended.whyKo: Washington Post 인터뷰에서 언급됨; Rand가 Kalanick의 개인 성취와 자본주의 철학에 미친 영향의 추가 증거

recommended.title: Alexander Hamilton
recommended.author: Ron Chernow
recommended.why: Kalanick was so influenced he changed his Twitter avatar to Alexander Hamilton; reflects admiration for ambitious, entrepreneurial leadership
recommended.whyKo: Kalanick이 너무 영향을 받아 Twitter 아바타를 Alexander Hamilton으로 변경함; 야심찬 기업가적 리더십에 대한 존경을 반영

recommended.title: The Art of War
recommended.author: Sun Tzu
recommended.why: Became Kalanick's 'bible'; evident in his military-style competitive approach to business and strategic thinking
recommended.whyKo: Kalanick의 '성경'이 됨; 사업에 대한 군사적 경쟁 접근과 전략적 사고에 명백함

recommended.title: Ender's Game
recommended.author: Orson Scott Card
recommended.why: Kalanick described as 'About a kid trained by military to play video games but realizes at end he was in actual war'; resonates with strategic simulation of competitive business
recommended.whyKo: Kalanick이 '군대에서 훈련받은 아이가 비디오 게임을 하지만 마지막에 실제 전쟁에 있었다는 것을 깨닫는 이야기'라고 설명; 경쟁 사업의 전략적 시뮬레이션과 공명

personalTraits.knownFor: Brilliant but controversial founder; aggressive expansion strategy; 'move fast and break things' philosophy; cutthroat competitive approach; intense demanding leadership; visionary for ridesharing disruption; resilience through business failures; ultimately undone by toxic workplace culture; known for intensity, drive, and determination but also for enabling harmful corporate environment
personalTraits.knownForKo: 뛰어나지만 논쟁적인 창업가; 공격적인 확장 전략; '빠르게 움직이고 것들을 부수자'는 철학; 냉정한 경쟁적 접근; 강렬하고 까다로운 리더십; 라이드 쉐어링 혁신의 선구자; 사업 실패를 통한 회복력; 결국 독성 직장 문화에 의해 무너짐; 강렬함, 추진력, 결단력으로 알려졌지만 또한 해로운 기업 환경을 조성한 것으로도 알려짐
personalTraits.philanthropy: Relatively low-profile charitable activities compared to business ventures. Supports education and economic opportunity initiatives through 10100 investment fund. Focus areas include education and emerging markets development. Personal charitable giving appears limited compared to entrepreneurial activities. CloudKitchens business model focused on creating infrastructure rather than explicit philanthropic mission.
personalTraits.philanthropyKo: 사업 활동에 비해 상대적으로 낮은 자선 활동. 10100 투자 펀드를 통해 교육 및 경제 기회 이니셔티브 지원. 주요 초점은 교육 및 신흥 시장 개발. 개인적 자선 기부는 기업가 활동에 비해 제한적으로 보임. CloudKitchens 비즈니스 모델은 명시적인 자선 미션보다는 기반 시설 구축에 초점
personalTraits.controversies: Most significant: 2017 sexual harassment and workplace discrimination scandal. Susan Fowler's viral blog post detailed sexual harassment on first day of work; Perkins Coie investigation found 215 complaints including 54 discrimination cases, 47 sexual harassment, 45 unprofessional behavior, 33 bullying. Executive Amit Singhal resigned after failing to disclose harassment claim from Google; Eric Alexander fired for mishandling rape investigation in India. Kalanick instructed PR team to blame media for suggesting Uber liability in 2013 choking incident. Video surfaced of him berating Uber driver. Uber under his leadership used kill switches during police raids; attempted regulatory lobbying. His leadership created culture of aggressive, cutthroat competition that deprioritized ethical concerns.
personalTraits.controversiesKo: 가장 심각한 것: 2017년 성희롱 및 직장 차별 스캔들. Susan Fowler의 바이럴 블로그 게시물이 첫 번째 근무일에 성희롱을 상세히 설명; Perkins Coie 조사에서 차별 54건, 성희롱 47건, 부적절한 행동 45건, 괴롭힘 33건 등 총 215건의 민원 발견. Executive Amit Singhal은 Google의 성희롱 청구를 공개하지 않아 사임; Eric Alexander는 인도의 강간 조사 부실 처리로 해고. Kalanick은 PR팀에 2013년 질식 사건에서 Uber의 책임을 시사한 언론을 탓하도록 지시. 그가 Uber 운전자를 꾸짖는 영상이 나옴. 그의 리더십 하의 Uber는 경찰 급습 중에 킬 스위치를 사용; 규제 로비 시도. 그의 리더십은 윤리적 우려를 경시한 공격적이고 냉정한 경쟁의 문화를 조성함.

pivotalMentors.name: Garrett Camp
pivotalMentors.relationship: Co-founder of Uber; met at LeWeb conference 2008; collaborative partnership rather than traditional mentor relationship
pivotalMentors.influence: Camp was idea originator and inspired Kalanick to join Uber with ambitious vision. Kalanick impressed Camp with his determination and courage, making Camp recruit him as CEO. Together created Uber's initial strategy, though their leadership approaches differed significantly.

pivotalMentors.name: Previous founders (Scour, Red Swoosh experience)
pivotalMentors.relationship: Self-taught through entrepreneurial experience and failure
pivotalMentors.influence: Learned resilience from $250 billion lawsuit at Scour; developed persistence through Red Swoosh funding challenges; these experiences built his aggressive problem-solving style and willingness to take risks

additionalInsights.dailyRoutine: Known for intense, relentless work ethic; demands above-and-beyond performance from employees. Operates with military-commander mentality. Focuses on aggressive goal-hitting and target achievement. Limited public information on specific daily habits; appears to have maintained high-pressure lifestyle throughout Uber tenure. No documented morning routines or wellness practices like Ek; instead characterized by constant hustle mentality.
keyAnecdotes: Paris 2008: Stranded on cold, snowy night unable to hail taxi; this mundane frustration sparked the idea for Uber in conversation with Garrett Camp
Spent $800 hiring private driver on New Year's Eve, which seemed excessive but inspired thinking about on-demand transportation solutions
Initially reluctant about Uber despite Camp's persistence; skepticism eventually overcome by belief in concept, showing ability to be convinced by compelling vision
Video of berating Uber driver showed public display of aggressive behavior; later apologized but incident exemplified leadership style that tolerated disrespect
Susan Fowler's viral blog post exposed widespread harassment culture; moment when personal leadership failures became public reckoning and forced accountability
additionalInsights.turningPoint: The Paris taxi incident in 2008 during LeWeb conference, though potentially more creation myth than reality, represents the conceptual turning point. However, the true turning point came when he accepted CEO role at Uber in 2010 and drove aggressive global expansion. But the ultimate reversal came in 2017 when accumulation of sexual harassment allegations, workplace culture failures, and regulatory pressures forced resignation. This marked shift from unchecked power to accountability, transforming from Silicon Valley golden boy to cautionary tale about toxic leadership.
additionalInsights.managementStyle: Autocratic, centralized decision-making without team input or collaborative consultation. Maintains tight operational control and strategic direction. Aggressive growth prioritized over internal culture, employee welfare, or ethical considerations. Intense demands for exceeding targets and achieving goals. Military-commander metaphor: views role as general leading troops into competitive life-or-death business battles. 'Move fast and break things' mentality that became hallmark of early Uber and Silicon Valley. Rewards results above all else; systematically deprioritizes internal concerns and employee welfare. Encourages challenging regulations and entering gray markets. Stifles internal dissent. Competitive approach treats business as warfare with winners and losers. Toe-stepping, cutthroat meritocracy that forced departure of those unable to match intensity. Later recognized need for fundamental change but years of enabling toxic culture caused massive damage.
